summary: The main principal-duality theorem states an actual equivalence (W^k)^! ≃ W^{k'}, while the later 'precise statement' says the dual is not W^{k'} itself, only a chiral CE algebra with the same kappa. This is a foundational inversion-vs-dual conflation.
title: Principal W-duality surface is internally contradictory

summary: The proof of thm:w-koszul-precise proves the critical-level package and skips Part B. The theorem still asserts a general-level statement, so the claim surface currently outruns the written proof.
title: W precise statement leaves its general-level claim unproved

summary: thm:explicit-theta asserts the full kappa·eta⊗Lambda formula under a one-channel hypothesis, but later results and concordance explicitly restrict that formula to the proved uniform-weight lane and record failure of tautological purity in multi-weight settings.
title: Explicit theta theorem overclaims beyond the proved scalar lane

summary: prop:subexponential-growth-automatic assumes only positive energy and finite-dimensional graded pieces, then imports Hardy-Ramanujan-Rademacher/effective-central-charge asymptotics. Those asymptotics need much stronger modularity/character-control input, so the proposition is overstated.
title: Sub-exponential growth is not automatic from positive energy alone

summary: thm:yangian-koszul-dual is stated as an exact RTT duality theorem, but the proof leans on a 16x16 sl2 orthogonal-complement computation and then declares higher R^{-1} coefficients irrelevant because the mode expansion sees only leading-order data. That does not justify the full algebra-level identification.
title: Yangian duality proof only controls leading-order / low-rank data

summary: thm:w3-koszul-dual imports the principal W-duality theorem as if it proved (W_3^k)^! ≃ W_3^{k'}. Since the parent theorem surface is internally contradictory, the specialized W3 theorem is downstream-fragile as written.
title: Derived W3 duality theorem inherits the contradictory W-duality surface
